sprint.goal: Harden enforcement runtime — fix all P0/P1 governance bugs before dogfood run
sprint.focus: Security correctness: govern-shell.sh JSON safety, cmdEvaluate bypass, bounded-execution policy, test coverage baseline

p0.title: Critical: bounded-execution policy denies ALL run_shell calls in enforce mode
bug: cmdEvaluate silently ignores JSON unmarshal error — governance bypass
bug: govern-shell.sh unescaped $COMMAND in printf — silently defaults to allow

p1.title: bug: governance policy gap — plain rm and rm -r not blocked by no-destructive-rm
bug: classifyShellRisk prefix matching too broad — false read-only classification
bug: govern-shell.sh uses fragile sed to parse JSON
test: zero test coverage across all packages
bug: stale crush references in cmd/shellforge/main.go

p2.title: bug: scheduler.go silently ignores os.WriteFile error
bug: dead code in flattenParams() overwrites result before using it
bug: filepath.Glob with ** in cmdScan never matches any Go files
agentguard.yaml misleading comment says monitor but mode is enforce
Dogfood: run ShellForge swarm on jared box via RunPod GPU

p3.title: [research] lean-ctx — 88% token reduction via shell hook + MCP server
[research] nono — kernel-enforced agent sandbox via macOS Seatbelt
[research] omlx — SSD KV caching doubles swarm capacity

capability_gaps: No dev-agent in swarm — P0 bugs require human or feature-dev dispatch to write fixes and open PRs
notes: Run 2 (2026-03-29T10:00Z): Triaged new issue #77 as P3. Assigned security-scanner to P0 security bugs (#62, #75) and P1 rm gap (#69) + sed fragility (#67). Assigned qa-agent to P0 policy logic bug (#58) and P1 risk classifier (#63) + test coverage (#68). PR budget still 0/3 — dev-agent needed to convert analysis to PRs.
